filter_pushdown.note: lineitem is sorted by l_shipdate — binary search finds first/last qualifying block; ~84% of blocks pruned. Interior blocks skip per-row date check.
Discount zone map applied on candidate blocks only; minimal pruning power since lineitem is not sorted by discount.
Quantity zone map applied on candidate blocks; skip block if block_min >= 24.0. Moderate pruning benefit.

parallelism.note: Qualifying blocks [first_block, last_block] partitioned evenly across 64 threads. Each thread accumulates a local double sum. Final reduce is a sequential sum of 64 thread-local values — no locks or atomics needed.

index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=600][int32_t min, int32_t max per block]
index_usage.detail: Binary search for first block where block_max >= 8766 and last block where block_min < 9131. All blocks outside [first_block, last_block] are pruned (~84% skip rate). For interior blocks where block_min >= 8766 && block_max < 9131, omit per-row shipdate check in inner loop.

index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=600][double min, double max per block]
index_usage.detail: Within candidate block range, skip block if block_max < 0.05 || block_min > 0.07. Benefit is low within date range since discount is not sorted, but cost is negligible.

index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=600][double min, double max per block]
index_usage.detail: Within candidate block range, skip block if block_min >= 24.0. Moderate pruning since quantity values cluster.

operations: load 4 l_shipdate int32 → compare [8766, 9131) → mask (skipped for interior blocks)
load 4 l_extendedprice double → multiply by l_discount double
masked accumulate into SIMD accumulator register
horizontal reduce at end of block to scalar double
inner_loop_strategy.boundary_block_handling: per-row shipdate range check for first and last qualifying blocks
inner_loop_strategy.interior_block_optimization: omit shipdate predicate from mask — always true

phases.action: mmap all 3 zone map files (tiny: 600 blocks × 8-16 bytes each, <50 KB total); binary search on shipdate zone map to find [first_block=~78, last_block=~171]
madvise(MADV_WILLNEED) on qualifying byte ranges of all 4 columns in parallel across threads; covers only blocks [first_block, last_block] ≈ 15.6% of each column file
phases.estimated_bytes_prefetched_per_column: ~376 MB total for 4 columns × 94 blocks × 100000 rows (vs 1.86 GB for full scan of all 4 columns)

estimated_cost.notes: I/O limited to ~15.6% of column data. With 64-thread parallel sequential reads and madvise prefetch, I/O and compute overlap heavily. Scalar aggregate with thread-local accumulation has zero contention. Hot-cache runs are pure compute-bound (AVX2 fused filter+multiply-accumulate over ~9.4M rows on 64 cores ≈ 5ms).
